# [RISC-V] Miscompile on rv64gcv with -O3

**Author:** ewlu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/134126
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** backend:RISC-V, miscompilation, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-04-08T14:20:28Z

## Body

Testcase:
```c
long a;
long c;
signed char d = 2;
char e;
unsigned f;
short j;
long long ac;
int i = 2320478;
_Bool l = 1;
_Bool m;
int n = 9;
int q = 18004875;
unsigned u[15][15][15];
long long w[5][15];
void g(int i, short j, _Bool l, _Bool m, int n, int q,
       unsigned u[][15][15], long long w[][15]) {
  if (n) {
    for (signed char x = 0; x < (char)q + 132; x += i - 90)
      for (signed y = 0; y < 14; y += ~50 + 54) {
        c = w[x][x];
        d *= l && u[x][y][y];
        e = u[x][x][y];
      }
  }
  f += a ? m : 0;
}
int main() {
  for (int x = 0; x < 15; ++x)
    for (int y = 0; y < 15; ++y)
      for (int z = 0; z < 15; ++z)
        u[x][y][z] = 807197787;
  g(i, j, l, m, n, q, u, w);
  ac = d;
  __builtin_printf("%llu\n", ac);
}
```

Commands
```
# riscv
$ /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/clang -march=rv64gcv_zvl256b -flto -O3 ./red.c -o user-config.out -fsigned-char -fno-strict-aliasing -fwrapv
$ QEMU_CPU=rv64,vlen=256,rvv_ta_all_1s=true,rvv_ma_all_1s=true,v=true,vext_spec=v1.0,zve32f=true,zve64f=true timeout --verbose -k 0.1 4 /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/qemu-riscv64 user-config.out 1
0

$ /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/clang -march=rv64gcv_zvl256b -flto -O2 ./red.c -o user-config.out -fsigned-char -fno-strict-aliasing -fwrapv 
$ QEMU_CPU=rv64,vlen=256,rvv_ta_all_1s=true,rvv_ma_all_1s=true,v=true,vext_spec=v1.0,zve32f=true,zve64f=true timeout --verbose -k 0.1 4 /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/qemu-riscv64 user-config.out 1
2

# x86
$ ./native.out
2
```

bisected to 25e43334a86804c77d460493e37b57274257461a as the first bad commit

Tried reducing testcase more by eliminating dead code but that causes the failure to disappear. Also at some point during manual reduction, -O2 also started to fail but now it's generating expected output. If anyone wants, I can provide the unreduced testcase with the -O2 failure as well.

Found via fuzzer.

long a;

    for (signed char x = 0; x &lt; (char)q + 132; x += i - 90)

### Comment 5 - preames

Just to note - I tried to reproduce this today without LTO, and failed.  I'll try again later today with LTO, and see if I can extract a triggering IR example to share.

### Comment 6 - bababuck

I noticed the same behavior, tried to reproduce without  `-flto` on Spike and was unable to.

---

### Comment 7 - preames

I've identified the cause of the failure.  Patch forthcoming.  

---

### Comment 8 - preames

The bug is that we're matching the isSpanSplatShuffle predicate as if undef elements in the mask can be rewritten as late non-undef elements, but then we actually use the original mask without rewriting.  We appear to have the same bug in isLocalRepeatingShuffle.

Thankfully, neither of the changes seem to have made it into release branches.  

Illustrative bad match: t208: v16i32 = vector_shuffle<u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,u,3,2,1,0,u> t232, undef:v16i32


---

### Comment 9 - bababuck

Any idea why this required LTO to occur?

---

### Comment 10 - preames

> Any idea why this required LTO to occur?

It looks like we vectorize more aggressively for LTO.  

---
